full_prompt: Design a machined 6061-T6 aluminum housing for a 3 kg electronics payload (single-board computer + power supply) to pass MIL-STD-810H Method 514.8 Procedure I vibration and Method 516.8 Procedure I shock in a ground-vehicle transit environment, plus Method 501.6 high-temperature storage 71 C and Method 502.6 low-temperature operation -40 C. Envelope: 200 x 150 x 80 mm internal; external up to 220 x 170 x 100 mm including flange. Interfaces: four M6 clearance holes on 190 x 140 mm rectangular footprint for mounting to ground-vehicle rack; one D38999 Series III 19-11 connector cutout on the short face; removable lid with 10 x M4 captive screws and EPDM gasket for IP67 sealing. Material: aluminum 6061-T6 (yield 276 MPa, UTS 310 MPa, Young's modulus 68.9 GPa, density 2700 kg/m^3, CTE 23.6e-6/K). Load cases per MIL-STD-810H: LC1 Method 514.8 Annex D Cat 20 ground-vehicle common carrier PSD: 0.04 g^2/Hz at 5-10 Hz, ramp down -6 dB/oct to 500 Hz, 0.001 g^2/Hz flat to 2000 Hz, total ~2.2 g RMS (typical common-carrier value); LC2 Method 516.8 Procedure I functional shock: 20 g peak sawtooth 11 ms (FSOP); LC3 Method 516.8 Procedure V crash hazard: 40 g peak 11 ms; LC4 Method 501.6 high-temperature 71 C storage 4 hours; LC5 Method 502.6 low-temperature -40 C 4 hours operation; LC6 random vibration durability test 12 hours per axis at LC1 PSD (accelerated life). Pass/fail: (R1) random vibration 3-sigma von Mises under LC1 PSD <= yield / 1.5 = 184 MPa (safety factor 1.5 on yield for non-primary DoD structure); (R2) shock LC2 peak von Mises <= UTS / 1.25 = 248 MPa; (R3) shock LC3 crash-hazard peak <= UTS = 310 MPa (no rupture); (R4) lid sealing gasket compression retention under LC1 vibration + LC4/LC5 thermal cycling: gasket seat gap variation <= 0.2 mm (IP67 retention); (R5) first natural mode >= 200 Hz (standard avoidance of ground-vehicle input energy band 5-200 Hz); (R6) thermal-induced stress at delta-T 111 K (from -40 to 71 C) <= 184 MPa; (R7) mass <= 1.8 kg. Deliverables: STEP AP242 + IGES; FEA with PSD random, transient shock, thermal-structural; modal analysis; gasket compression verification.
short_prompt:
Design a machined 6061-T6 aluminum housing for a 3 kg electronics payload (single-board computer + power supply) to pass MIL-STD-810H Method 514.8 Procedure I vibration and Method 516.8 Procedure I shock in a ground-vehicle transit environment, plus Method 501.6 high-temperature storage 71 C and Method 502.6 low-temperature operation -40 C.

Envelope: 200 x 150 x 80 mm internal; external up to 220 x 170 x 100 mm including flange. Interfaces: four M6 clearance holes on 190 x 140 mm to the vehicle rack; one D38999 Series III 19-11 connector cutout on the short face; removable lid with 10 x M4 captive screws and EPDM gasket for IP67.

Material: 6061-T6.

Load cases per MIL-STD-810H.
- LC1 Method 514.8 Annex D Cat 20 ground-vehicle common-carrier PSD: 0.04 g^2/Hz at 5-10 Hz, ramp down -6 dB/oct to 500 Hz, 0.001 g^2/Hz flat to 2000 Hz, total ~2.2 g RMS.
- LC2 Method 516.8 Procedure I functional shock: 20 g peak sawtooth 11 ms.
- LC3 Method 516.8 Procedure V crash hazard: 40 g peak 11 ms.
- LC4 Method 501.6 high-temperature 71 C storage 4 hours.
- LC5 Method 502.6 low-temperature -40 C 4 hours operation.
- LC6 random vibration durability: 12 hours per axis at LC1 PSD.

Gates include first natural mode >= 200 Hz; IP67 gasket seat-gap variation <= 0.2 mm; thermal-induced stress at delta-T 111 K bounded by yield-derived allowable; mass <= 1.8 kg.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: IP67 is a physical ingress test, not simulated.

prompt.title: 6061-T6 MIL-STD-810H vibration/shock/thermal qualified electronics enclosure
prompt.brief: Design a 220x170x100 mm 6061-T6 enclosure for a 3 kg payload passing MIL-STD-810H Methods 514.8, 516.8, 501.6, 502.6.

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: IP67 retention across vibration + thermal cycling

analysis_types: modal + random PSD + transient shock + thermal-structural

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: IP67 verified physically; gasket model approximates sealing.

notes.numeric_source: MIL-STD-810H Method 514.8 Annex D Cat 20 ground-vehicle PSD; Method 516.8 Procedures I and V shock pulses; Methods 501.6 / 502.6 temperature limits.

eval_coverage: random PSD + transient shock + thermal trifecta
